requirement: Buyer can create a sourcing event for approved suppliers.
Buyer can manage and track sourcing events in real-time.
Buyer can evaluate and award contracts to suppliers.
Buyer can manage and track supplier collaboration and communication.
Buyer can access and view sourcing event history and analytics.
Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Buyer can manage and track purchase orders in real-time.
Buyer can access and view purchase order history and analytics.
Buyer can create a sourcing request form with required information.
Buyer can initiate a sourcing event (RFx or auction) based on the sourcing request form.
Supplier can participate and respond to sourcing events (RFx or auction) via email.
Buyer can evaluate and compare supplier responses for sourcing events.
Buyer can award a supplier for sourcing events.
Supplier can be onboarded if they are new.
Advanced reporting capabilities offer actionable insights into sourcing events, supplier performance, and spend data.
Procurement Buyer can initiate a sourcing event in one of three ways: from scratch, from an existing template, or by copying from a previously conducted event.
Procurement Buyer can configure event settings, including event name, currency, terms and conditions, and sourcing team.
Procurement Buyer can add suppliers to the sourcing event and set evaluations.
Procurement Buyer can view and manage event timelines, including event start and end dates, and supplier response deadlines.
Procurement Buyer can track cost avoidance and planned savings for the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can select the type of sourcing event, including RFx stage, allow multiple responses, and hide supplier responses.
Procurement Buyer can attach terms and conditions or other relevant documents to the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can convert the sourcing event into a sealed bid or unsealed bid by selecting the Unseal manually option.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event with event details, timelines, and attachments.
Procurement Buyer can add items to the sourcing event with item details and specifications.
Procurement Buyer can add suppliers to the sourcing event from the Coupa supplier list or by manually entering supplier details.
Procurement Buyer can add evaluators to the sourcing event manually.
Procurement Buyer can configure event timelines and invite internal team members to conduct specific tasks.
Procurement Buyer can create and manage sourcing events, including setting evaluation weights, criteria, and evaluators.
Procurement Buyer can publish sourcing events to suppliers and send invitations via email with a system-generated OTP.
Suppliers can accept terms and conditions, view event information, and participate in the event by answering questions.
Suppliers can resolve queries via a chat window and communicate with the Procurement Buyer through a live communication channel.
Procurement Buyer can edit event details, including weights, planned savings, and currency, but changes to terms and conditions and event type settings will create a new revised event.
Procurement Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Suppliers can submit their responses to the event, including mandatory information.
Procurement Buyer can view the list of suppliers who were invited to the event and the ones who have responded.
Procurement Buyer can send email reminders to suppliers.
Procurement Buyer can extend the timeline of the bid by editing the event and resubmitting the production event.
Event gets auto-closed or Procurement Buyer can manually end the event by clicking on 'End Event'.
Procurement Buyer can create a follow-up event by clicking on the Create Follow-on Event button with the final shortlisted suppliers.
Procurement Buyer can view the responses received by selecting the event and navigating to the Responses Section.
Procurement Buyer can request internal users for evaluation (technical and commercial).
Procurement Buyer can finalize the supplier to be awarded based on the supplier responses.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event for approved suppliers.
Evaluators can add their scores and evaluations for the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can review evaluator's evaluations and award the selected winner(s) in the sourcing event.
The event status remains as Pending Evaluation until all invited Evaluators have submitted their evaluations, or the evaluation process has ended.
Once approvals are completed, the event is marked as awarded and the supplier is awarded the event.
Procurement Buyer can flip the Sourcing event into a new Requisition or Purchase Order.
Procurement Buyer can create a new requisition and submit it for approval.
Procurement Buyer can initiate the sourcing process by flipping a PR to Sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can create a Sourcing Event from scratch, using a template, or copying from a previous event.
Procurement Buyer can configure event settings, timelines, details, add suppliers, and set evaluations for a Sourcing Event.
Procurement Buyer can select a specific commodity to be procured/sourced when creating a Sourcing Event.
Procurement Buyer can filter specific templates/events by selecting a specific commodity to be procured/sourced.
Procurement Buyer can create a sourcing event with various options such as RFx stage, event type, and terms and conditions.
Procurement Buyer can configure event details such as attachments, forms, and items.
Procurement Buyer can configure timelines for the sourcing event, including event time zone, start, and end dates.
Procurement Buyer can invite internal team members to participate in the sourcing event and assign tasks.
Supplier can bid in different currencies and suppliers can add attachments while communicating on message centre.
Procurement Buyer can manage event team, including adding event owner, watcher, and evaluator.
Procurement Buyer can add additional items to a purchase order by clicking on ‘Add New’ and selecting ‘New Item’.
Procurement Buyer can manually enter or adjust item prices as necessary.
Procurement Buyer can add specification with respect to items in the item details field.
Procurement Buyer can add suppliers to the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can manually add evaluators for the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can assign weights to specific categories of the sourcing event.
Procurement Buyer can choose specific evaluators for technical and commercial evaluations.
Procurement Buyer can launch the event and make it visible to suppliers.
Procurement Buyer can edit the event after it has been launched, except for changes in currency, Terms and Conditions, and Event Type Settings.
Supplier receives an invitation email for participating in the event and viewing event details.
Supplier accepts the terms and conditions before participating in the event.
Supplier can access and participate in the event after accepting the terms and conditions.
Supplier can resolve queries via the chat window.
Supplier submits their responses to the event.
Supplier can export the questionnaire to Excel and import responses from Excel.
Supplier can view the history of changes made to the form and save their responses.
Procurement Buyer can send email reminders to the suppliers.
Procurement Buyer can extend the timeline of the bid.
Event gets auto-closed or Procurement Buyer can manually end the event after which the bidding closes and the event gets sealed.
Procurement Buyer can create a follow-on event for RFP or RFQ or Auction.
Procurement Buyer can evaluate supplier responses and request internal users for evaluation.
Evaluators can add scores, evaluations, and comments, and submit their evaluations.
The Procurement Team can review evaluator’s evaluations.
The Procurement Team can award the selected winner(s) in the Sourcing event using award option.
Once approvals are completed, mark the event as awarded.
The Procurement Buyer can award the selected winner(s) in the Sourcing event using award option.
The Procurement Buyer will flip the awarded vendor from the Sourcing event back to the originating PR.
The Procurement Buyer can initiate a new Contract or new Catalog Item for the awarded Supplier.
Users can leverage three distinct types of reverse auctions within Coupa.
Users can conduct English Reverse Auctions within Coupa.
Users can conduct Dutch Reverse Auctions within Coupa.
Users can conduct Japanese Reverse Auctions within Coupa.
Buyer can create an auction event from scratch, using an event template, or by copying a previous event.
Buyer can configure auction-specific settings, including English Reverse Auction, and set up automatic bid extensions.
Buyer can select which cost to compare the new quote to, and set up bidding rules to ensure fair and efficient price development.
Buyer can provide feedback to suppliers, including the currently best price per item or lot, to help them improve their bids.
Buyer can create an auction event with a timeline, including a period between event start and bidding start, and an RFx phase to collect additional information.
Buyer can monitor the bidding process in the competitive chart and follow the changes during the auction.
Buyer can remove bids on behalf of suppliers and record the removal in the event history.
Buyer can prevent inadvertently incorrect submissions by showing a warning message when a supplier attempts to submit a quote which is more than 50% lower than the previous one.
Buyer can award the selected supplier after approval and implement the award in the same way as any event in Coupa Sourcing.
Buyer can define savings types, including cost avoidance and planned savings, and attach supporting documents.
System auto-calculates Negotiated Savings based on Base price, Bid price, and Quantity.
Buyer can configure settings for sourcing events, such as requiring suppliers to login, allowing multiple responses, and sealing bids.
Buyer can enable or disable features such as Coupa Sourcing Advantage, Supplier Community Search, and public events.
System enforces maximum line items, suppliers, and settings for sourcing events to avoid impaired performance.
Suppliers can access and participate in public sourcing events created by the company.
Coupa helps users find and connect with fellow users in the Coupa Community to share experiences of sourcing for a particular commodity.
Users can manage individual users' access to content at the department level using content groups.
Users can run 'what if' scenarios to support the award decision.
